finding_results.notes: The opening now starts on fresh consequence: Petra arrives with new intake forms immediately, the two new cases are active before the scope explanation fully unfolds, and the facility-wide reporting lockdown is shown on the page as a public corridor crisis rather than deferred orientation.

Kai's hesitation is now motivated by Lena's clinically composed response during the episode, then the delay becomes an explicit negotiated choice: Lena names the concrete institutional cost of reporting, Kai sets a threshold for escalation, and the corridor beat states why she keeps the card instead of filing it.
Chapter 6 now contains a single glasses reference, and the remove-rub-replace gesture does not recur elsewhere in the chapter.
All chapter_06 references now use the accented form 'Tomás.' The packet's manuscript-wide inconsistency still requires changes in other chapters, which were out of scope for this pass.
Chapter_06 now uses the continuity-sheet canonical location 'base of her skull' for Lena's port references. Remaining off-sheet instances in other chapters were outside the allowed edit scope.
The chapter now meets the floor at 4000 words exactly while preserving the heading contract and adding causally necessary consequence/withholding material instead of filler.

Removed narrator assertion ('The professional polish in his voice was cracking now. More Birmingham came through when he spoke fast.') and replaced with audible stress markers in dialogue. Line 33 adds colloquial connector ('Right, so') and British phrasing ('has got'). Line 37 introduces two self-corrections: an interrupted restart ('enough to cause— intermittent disruption, right') and a mid-clause correction ('it's not— it hasn't cascaded'), plus colloquial 'firmware's got.' Line 41 adds British profanity ('bloody'), informal shortening ('the bay'), colloquial verb ('hooking them up'), and private-register connector ('So'). Petra's lines remain clinically steady, making the register contrast audible without narrator instruction.

Replaced the four abstract pause-label beats with local physical aftermath: Petra's control ritual in the bay, Lena's reflexive check of her port, the room's medical tick-over during the lull with Kai, and the call-button/card geometry after the nurse exits.

pass_summaries.summary: Rebuilt the opening around fresh cases and immediate lockdown consequence, made Lena's blackout concealment a legible and costly choice, brought the chapter to the 4000-word floor, and cleaned the chapter-local recurrence/continuity defects. The two manuscript-wide continuity findings are only partial here because this pass was restricted to chapter_06.

Single finding addressed. Yusuf's stress register in the Petra exchange now surfaces through dialogue mechanics — interrupted restarts, self-corrections, colloquial connectors, and a British profanity marker — rather than through narrator assertion. The register contrast between Yusuf's cracking composure and Petra's clinical steadiness is carried entirely by the speech itself. No changes to Lena/Kai or Margot sections; all other dialogue checked against style bible contraction levels and found character-true.
Revised all four cited pause beats so the scene pauses land through character-specific action, sensation, and room detail instead of repeated abstract silence labels.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=6, PARTIAL=2, UNRESOLVED=0).
